title: insight drift
summary: The thread explores insight drift, a phenomenon where observed patterns across multiple arcs are aggregated to identify recurring themes in code changes. Key patterns include Commit-subject vs. diff-scope understatement, Bundle-as-shape, and Bucket-vs-content asymmetry, with the latter addressing naming inconsistencies in CHANGELOGs. The catalogue assembled six patterns and sixteen instances, highlighting how drift affects narrative and documentation. The outcome is a structured understanding of drift patterns, emphasizing the importance of arc-side observation for future analysis.
